paper: Yang et al., M-GCLO: Multiple Ground Constrained LiDAR Odometry, ISPRS Annals X-1-2024
claim: Multiple ground point-to-plane constraints improve vertical and attitude stability when fused with non-ground NDT correspondences.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

variants.ground_on: default multiple-ground point-to-plane constraints plus non-ground NDT
variants.ground_off: --m-gclo-no-ground after --m-gclo-dense-profile; classified ground correspondences are held out of the optimizer

conclusion: The ground factor is not needed to minimize local translational RPE on KITTI seq00/07, but disabling it increases rotational drift and more than doubles ATE on both sequences. Under this protocol, M-GCLO's multiple-ground constraint behaves as a stability/anchoring term rather than a pure translational-drift reducer.
